topic: Explaining your research to someone outside your field
intro: Today’s speaking practice focuses on describing your research clearly, simply, and naturally.
speaking_lines: My research focuses on monitoring structural damage using sensing signals.
One of the main problems I am trying to solve is how to detect damage more efficiently.
I use data-driven methods together with sensing technologies to analyze signal patterns.
A challenge in this work is that different tasks may require different sensing settings.
In the long term, I want this research to support smarter and more automated monitoring systems.
practice_questions: How would you explain your research to a non-expert?
What is the main challenge in your current work?
Why do you think your research matters in practice?
